actionable_feedback: Resolve score-field inconsistency in grader metadata and attempt outputs: clearly distinguish deterministic artifact-contract results from model semantic scores and publish one canonical top-level score or an explicit explanation of multiple score fields (e.g., top_level_score with source='deterministic' or 'semantic', plus separate fields deterministic_score and semantic_score).

Make the .xlsx programmatically verifiable: ensure computed cells contain exported visible formula strings and/or include a separate 'formulas' sheet or formulas.txt export listing sheet/cell -> formula pairs.
Embed a machine-readable output_schema (JSON) in expert_memo.md (or as a separate artifact) describing expected sheets, column names, types, and the exact fields the acceptance checks will produce.

Add an acceptance_checks section (enumerated, automatable) that lists each check (Packet reproduction test, cell-formula presence checks, budget enforcement, penalty application tests) and sample code or commands to run them (e.g., python snippet requiring openpyxl).

Provide a programmatic snapshot (CSV or JSON) of final computed rows from the Summary sheet so verifiers can validate numeric results without relying on parsing the .xlsx in environments lacking openpyxl.

Add explicit row-level citations for derived values in the workbook (e.g., a citation column adjacent to computed fields linking back to the Packet row or option ID) so provenance is machine-checkable.

Re-run verification in an environment with openpyxl installed (or include the exported snapshots/formula text) and attach the verification log or updated grader output showing resolved score-field names.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: Good coverage of embedded packet, assumptions, representative formulas, intermediate values, and sensitivity checks. Missing machine-readable output_schema and an explicit acceptance_checks checklist with run instructions.

facility_location_2.xlsx: Workbook file present, but verifier could not parse it programmatically (openpyxl unavailable). Ensure computed cells include visible formulas or supply a separate formulas export and a CSV/JSON snapshot of the Summary sheet for reproducible verification.
feedback_summary: Deliverables are present and the memo documents the embedded packet, representative formulas, assumptions, and a justified provisional recommendation (BASE for all records). Major issues to address before final acceptance: (1) grader outputs contain inconsistent score fields (artifact_contract_score/ deterministic precheck = 1.0 vs model semantic final_score = 0.7) without clear canonical labeling or explanation; (2) the Excel workbook could not be parsed in the verification environment (openpyxl unavailable), so cell-formula presence and exact computed-value provenance were not programmatically verified; (3) the memo lacks an explicit machine-readable output_schema and a dedicated, enumerated acceptance_checks section that an automated verifier can run; (4) some derived-value row-level citations and a machine-readable snapshot of computed results are not provided, limiting reproducibility of automated checks. Fixing these items will enable full semantic verification and higher semantic score.

limitations: Grader could not parse facility_location_2.xlsx programmatically (openpyxl unavailable) and relied on expert_memo.md preview.
Multiple score fields in grader/verifier artifacts are inconsistent and should be clearly disambiguated.
recommended_next_steps: Resolve score-field inconsistencies and publish a canonical top-level score plus clearly labeled deterministic and semantic scores.
Add output_schema.json and acceptance_checks in expert_memo.md (or as separate artifacts) with runnable examples.
Expose formulas (formulas sheet or formulas.txt) and include a CSV/JSON snapshot of computed Summary rows.
